basic_production_proposal.title: The Breathing Wall
basic_production_proposal.logline: A vacant lot in Kensington becomes a living meditation space where walls learn to breathe with visitors, confronting them with the rhythms they've forgotten.
basic_production_proposal.narrative_concept:
In a neighborhood scarred by vacancy, a single lot transforms into an organism. Electrochromic walls pulse with a slow heartbeat rhythm, gradually syncing to the collective breathing of visitors inside. Over 60 minutes, the space evolves from hostile opacity to vulnerable transparency — the walls literally becoming see-through as trust builds between the space and its occupants.

The acoustic metamaterials shift from urban cacophony (sampled street sounds reflected back) to cathedral-like reverb, creating a sense of sacred space from industrial materials. Scent transitions from concrete dust to petrichor — the smell of rain on dry earth — marking the moment the space decides to welcome you.

This is not decoration. The walls ARE the character. Their journey from closed to open mirrors the neighborhood's own arc from abandonment to activation.
